text: Lesson 14
zh: 第14课

text: The Butterfly Effect
zh: 蝴蝶效应

text: Why do small errors make it impossible to predict the weather system with a high degree of accuracy?
zh: 为什么微小的误差会使人们无法高精度地预测天气系统？

text: Beyond two or three days, the world's best weather forecasts are speculative,
zh: 超过两三天，全球最好的天气预报也只是猜测，

text: and beyond six or seven they are worthless.
zh: 超过六七天，这些预报就毫无价值了。

text: The Butterfly Effect is the reason.
zh: 蝴蝶效应正是原因所在。

text: For small pieces of weather--
zh: 对于小尺度的天气现象而言——

text: and to a global forecaster, small can mean thunderstorms and blizzards--
zh: 对全球预报员来说，“小”可能意味着雷暴和暴风雪——

text: any prediction deteriorates rapidly.
zh: 任何预测都会迅速失效。

text: Errors and uncertainties multiply, cascading upward through a chain of turbulent features,
zh: 误差和不确定性不断累积，沿着一连串湍流特征向上扩散，

text: from dust devils and squalls up to continent-size eddies that only satellites can see.
zh: 从尘卷风、狂风一直到只有卫星才能观测到的、堪比大陆大小的涡旋。

text: The modern weather models work with a grid of points of the order of sixty miles apart,
zh: 现代天气模型使用的网格点间距大约为六十英里，

text: and even so, some starting data has to be guessed,
zh: 即便如此，由于地面站和卫星无法覆盖所有区域，

text: since ground stations and satellites cannot see everywhere.
zh: 一些初始数据仍需靠猜测得出。

text: But suppose the earth could be covered with sensors spaced one foot apart,
zh: 但假设地球表面能布满间隔一英尺的传感器，

text: rising at one-foot intervals all the way to the top of the atmosphere.
zh: 并且每隔一英尺向上延伸，一直到大气层顶部。

text: Suppose every sensor gives perfectly accurate readings of temperature,
zh: 假设每个传感器都能给出完全精确的温度读数，

text: pressure, humidity, and any other quantity a meteorologist would want.
zh: 以及气压、湿度和气象学家想要的任何其他数据。

text: Precisely at noon an infinitely powerful computer takes all the data and calculates what will happen at each point
zh: 正午时分，一台运算能力无限强大的计算机获取所有数据，计算出每一点在
12点01分、12点02分、12点03分……时会发生什么。

text: The computer will still be unable to predict whether Princeton, New Jersey, will have sun or rain on a day one month away.
zh: 这台计算机仍然无法预测，一个月后新泽西州普林斯顿是晴天还是雨天。

text: At noon the spaces between the sensors will hide fluctuations that the computer will not know about,
zh: 在正午时，传感器之间的空隙会掩盖计算机无法察觉的波动，

text: tiny deviations from the average.
zh: 也就是那些偏离平均值的微小偏差。

text: By 12.01, those fluctuations will already have created small errors one foot away.
zh: 到12点01分，这些波动已经在一英尺外造成了微小的误差。

text: Soon the errors will have multiplied to the ten-foot scale, and so on up to the size of the globe.
zh: 很快，这些误差就会扩大到十英尺的范围，并依此类推，一直扩展到全球的尺度。
